summary: Persona and memory migration is well-implemented with proper entry parsing, deduplication, character limit enforcement with overflow, and comprehensive test coverage for all 14 validation assertions (VAL-PERSONA-001 to 005, VAL-MEM-001 to 006, VAL-DOCS-001 to 003). The refactored merge logic using parseMemoryEntries/normalizeEntry is a significant improvement over the previous line-based dedup.

issues.description: The parseMemoryEntries method only splits on ## and ### headers plus horizontal rules. If a source uses other delimiters (e.g., blank lines with bullet lists), entries may not split correctly. This is a minor concern since the normalization still provides dedup at the content level.

ASSISTANT_NAME is set in .env inside migrateIdentity, but the feature description also mentions IDENTITY.md creation. The IDENTITY.md generation was already present in the core-script feature, so this is just an addition of the .env write, which is correct.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Migration tests use os.tmpdir() with unique subdirectories per test. This pattern avoids test interference and should be the standard for any future migration tests.
The migration script's updateEnvFile method preserves existing .env values when --overwrite is not set. This is a critical safety behavior that should be documented.

testCoverage.summary: Tests cover all persona assertions (VAL-PERSONA-001 to 005): SOUL.md copy, conflict skip, conflict overwrite with backup, IDENTITY.md creation, ASSISTANT_NAME in .env. Memory assertions (VAL-MEM-001 to 006): entry merging, deduplication, character limit overflow, USER.md merge, daily memory files, existing memory preservation. Docs assertions (VAL-DOCS-001 to 003): AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, PRINCIPLES.md.
